sn44.11:1.1: On one occasion Ven. Sabhiya Kaccāna was staying at Ñātika in the Brick Hall.
sn44.11:1.2: Then Vacchagotta the wanderer went to him and, on arrival, exchanged courteous greetings with him.
sn44.11:1.3: After an exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, he sat to one side. As he was sitting there, he said to Ven. Sabhiya Kaccāna,
sn44.11:1.4: “Now then, Master Kaccāna, does the Tathāgata exist after death?”
sn44.11:1.5: “Vaccha, that has not been declared by the Blessed One:
sn44.11:1.7: “Well then, Master Kaccāna, does the Tathāgata not exist after death?”
sn44.11:1.8: “Vaccha, that too has not been declared by the Blessed One:
sn44.11:1.9: ‘The Tathāgata does not exist after death.’”
sn44.11:2.1: “Then does the Tathāgata both exist and not exist after death?”
sn44.11:2.2: “That has not been declared by the Blessed One:
sn44.11:2.3: ‘The Tathāgata both exists and does not exist after death.’”
sn44.11:2.4: “Well then, does the Tathāgata neither exist nor not exist after death?”
sn44.11:2.5: “That too has not been declared by the Blessed One:
sn44.11:2.6: ‘The Tathāgata neither exists nor does not exist after death.’”
sn44.11:3.1: “Now, Master Kaccāna, when asked if the Tathāgata exists after death, you say,
sn44.11:3.2: ‘That has not been declared by the Blessed One:
sn44.11:3.4: When asked if the Tathāgata does not exist after death, you say,
sn44.11:3.5: ‘That too has not been declared by the Blessed One:
sn44.11:3.6: “The Tathāgata does not exist after death.”’
sn44.11:3.7: When asked if the Tathāgata both exists and does not exist after death, you say,
sn44.11:3.8: ‘That has not been declared by the Blessed One:
sn44.11:3.9: “The Tathāgata both exists and does not exist after death.”’
sn44.11:3.10: When asked if the Tathāgata neither exists nor does not exist after death, you say,
sn44.11:3.11: ‘That too has not been declared by the Blessed One:
sn44.11:3.12: “The Tathāgata neither exists nor does not exist after death.”’
sn44.11:3.13: Now, what is the cause, what is the reason, why that has not been declared by the contemplative Gotama?”
sn44.11:3.14: “Vaccha, whatever cause, whatever reason there would be for describing him as ‘possessed of form’ or ‘formless’ or ‘percipient’ or ‘non-percipient’ or ‘neither percipient nor non-percipient’: If that cause, that reason, were to cease totally everywhere, totally in every way without remainder,
sn44.11:3.15: then describing him by what means would one describe him as ‘possessed of form’ or ‘formless’ or ‘percipient’ or ‘non-percipient’ or ‘neither percipient nor non-percipient’?”
sn44.11:3.16: “How long has it been since you went forth, Master Kaccāna?”
sn44.11:3.18: “Whoever has gained just this much in this much time has gained a great deal, my friend—to say nothing of what he has thus gone beyond.”
